The **2021 CS [Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/Nebelhorn_Trophy "Nebelhorn Trophy")** was held on September 22–25, 2021 in [Oberstdorf, Germany](/wiki/Oberstdorf,_Germany "Oberstdorf, Germany").[[1]](#cite_note-announcement-1) It was part of the [2021–22 ISU Challenger Series](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_ISU_Challenger_Series "2021–22 ISU Challenger Series").[[2]](#cite_note-ISU-2390-2) It was also the final [qualifying event](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification "Figure skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics – Qualification") for the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics"). Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance").

In December 2020, the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union") designated Nebelhorn Trophy as the official figure skating qualification competition for the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics").[[3]](#cite_note-ISU-2362-3)[[4]](#cite_note-qualification-4) The [2009](/wiki/2009_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2009 Nebelhorn Trophy"), [2013](/wiki/2013_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2013 Nebelhorn Trophy"), and [2017](/wiki/2017_CS_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2017 CS Nebelhorn Trophy") editions of the event served the same role for the [2010](/wiki/2010_Winter_Olympics "2010 Winter Olympics"), [2014](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics"), and [2018 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2018_Winter_Olympics "2018 Winter Olympics"), respectively.[[5]](#cite_note-IFS201217-5)

## Olympic qualification event

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2021_CS_Nebelhorn_Trophy&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Olympic qualification event")]

Main article: [Figure skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics – Qualification](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification "Figure skating at the 2022 Winter Olympics – Qualification")

Initially, a total of six spots per singles event, three spots in pairs, and four spots in ice dance were available at the Nebelhorn Trophy. One additional quota spot became available in men's singles following the [2021 World Championships](/wiki/2021_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2021 World Figure Skating Championships").[[18]](#cite_note-ISU-2388-18) Available spots were allocated in descending order of placement among eligible nations.

Only [ISU member nations](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") that had not already earned an entry to the Olympics via the 2021 World Championships were allowed to attempt to earn a qualification berth at Nebelhorn Trophy. However, if a country earned two or three spots at the World Championships, but did not have two or three skaters, respectively, qualify for the free skating/dance, then they were allowed to send a skater/team who did not qualify for the free segment at World Championships to Nebelhorn Trophy to qualify the remaining spot.[[4]](#cite_note-qualification-4) The following ISU member nations were eligible to qualify a second or third spot in the listed disciplines at Nebelhorn Trophy:[[18]](#cite_note-ISU-2388-18)

| Spot # | Men | Women | Pairs | Dance |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 3 | [United States](/wiki/United_States_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "United States at the 2022 Winter Olympics")  [ROC](/wiki/Russian_Olympic_Committee_athletes_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Russian Olympic Committee athletes at the 2022 Winter Olympics")[[a]](#cite_note-RUS-19) | [United States](/wiki/United_States_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "United States at the 2022 Winter Olympics") | [China](/wiki/China_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "China at the 2022 Winter Olympics") | —N/a |
| 2 | [Canada](/wiki/Canada_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Canada at the 2022 Winter Olympics")  [France](/wiki/France_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "France at the 2022 Winter Olympics")  [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "South Korea at the 2022 Winter Olympics") | [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Belgium at the 2022 Winter Olympics")  [Austria](/wiki/Austria_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Austria at the 2022 Winter Olympics") | [Japan](/wiki/Japan_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Japan at the 2022 Winter Olympics") | [Italy](/wiki/Italy_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Italy at the 2022 Winter Olympics")  [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics "Great Britain at the 2022 Winter Olympics") |
| All other ISU member nations not previously qualified were eligible to earn one quota spot. | | | | |
